skill.name: prompt-template-designer
skill.description: Design reusable prompt templates for recurring AI tasks. Use it to extract stable patterns, define parameters, set constraints, and document success criteria.
skill.summary: Create structured, reusable prompt templates from repeated AI workflows.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backtick execution and weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but review found only markdown fences, template placeholders, prose, and checklist text in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network access, filesystem access, credential handling, obfuscation, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections are markdown code fences or escaped fences used to show prompt template examples. They do not execute commands and contain no shell invocation logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown fences, escaped fences, or inline placeholders in documentation. No evidence found of executable command dispatch, shell interpolation, or runtime code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections are substrings inside normal words, metadata, headings, placeholders, or checklist text. No cryptographic API or algorithm selection appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prompt-design documentation, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA-1, cipher configuration, hashing calls, or security-sensitive crypto behavior.

content.value_statement: Repeated AI prompts become hard to maintain when patterns stay informal. This skill helps turn proven prompts into reusable templates with clear parameters and success criteria.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether a repeated prompt pattern is worth turning into a template.
Separates invariant prompt structure from variable parameters.
Defines parameter types, examples, and required fields.
Organizes templates with intent, constraints, and success criteria.
Adds filled examples and common mistake sections for reuse.
Encourages versioning and effectiveness tracking for template quality.
limitations: It is not intended for one-time prompts or simple questions.
It does not generate or execute code by itself.
It requires human judgment to identify useful repeated patterns.
It does not validate templates against live model performance automatically.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Prompts
use_cases.description: Convert repeated team prompts into shared templates with parameters, examples, and success criteria.

use_cases.title: Document AI Workflows
use_cases.description: Capture recurring AI workflows so teammates can reuse them without rebuilding the prompt each time.

use_cases.title: Improve Prompt Quality
use_cases.description: Refine successful prompts into structured templates that reduce ambiguity and repeated revisions.

prompt_templates.title: Check Template Fit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before creating a template.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this repeated AI task: [describe task]. Decide if it should become a reusable prompt template. Check recurrence, decision points, domain knowledge, and expected reuse.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Parameters
prompt_templates.scenario: Identify stable structure and variable inputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these similar prompts: [paste examples]. Separate invariant instructions from variable inputs. Propose parameter names, types, required status, and short examples.

prompt_templates.title: Create Reusable Template
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a complete template from a proven pattern.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this recurring prompt pattern into a reusable template: [pattern]. Include when to use it, parameters, intent, constraints, success criteria, a filled example, and common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Template Maturity
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for a template library.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this prompt template: [template]. Assess clarity, parameter design, reuse scope, success metrics, versioning needs, and risks from over-parameterization or vague constraints.

output_examples.input: A team repeatedly asks an AI assistant to write commit messages from change summaries and ticket identifiers.
output_examples.output: Recommended template: commit message generator. Parameters should include change summary, ticket identifier, scope, and change type. Success criteria should include style compliance and clear business reason.

output_examples.input: A user wants to template a one-time request to explain a specific API behavior.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: do not create a template yet. The task lacks repeated use and does not justify maintenance overhead.

output_examples.input: A support team has several similar prompts for summarizing customer reports.
output_examples.output: Recommended next step: compare examples, identify required report fields, define output sections, and add quality checks for completeness and tone.

best_practices: Create a template only after the pattern has been used at least twice.
Keep parameters specific, typed, and limited to the values that truly vary.
Include a filled example so users can see the intended result.
anti_patterns: Creating templates for one-off prompts before a real pattern exists.
Adding too many parameters until the template becomes harder than the original prompt.
Omitting success criteria, which makes template quality hard to measure.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when you have repeated a similar AI prompt at least twice and want to make the pattern reusable.

faq.question: Can it help with simple one-off prompts?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly discourages templating simple or unique prompts because maintenance cost can exceed the benefit.

faq.question: What does a good template include?
faq.answer: A good template includes trigger conditions, parameters, constraints, success criteria, a filled example, and common mistakes.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is markdown guidance for prompt design and does not contain executable automation.

faq.question: How many parameters should a template use?
faq.answer: The skill recommends three to seven parameters. More parameters may mean the pattern should be split.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The template design guidance is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
